name: Airbyte MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Airbyte MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Airbyte's data integration platform — managing connections, triggering syncs, querying connector status, and monitoring data pipeline health.
use_cases: Triggering data sync jobs from data pipeline automation agents
Checking sync status and failure details for data quality agents
Managing Airbyte connections and their sync schedules
Querying available connectors for data integration planning agents
Monitoring data freshness across multiple source/destination pairs
not_for: Teams using Fivetran, Stitch, or other ETL platforms
Custom transformation logic (use dbt for that)
best_when: An agent needs to manage or monitor data pipeline syncs in an Airbyte-powered data integration environment.
avoid_when: You're using Fivetran, Stitch, or another ETL tool — use those providers' APIs.

auth.notes: Airbyte Cloud uses API key. Self-hosted uses basic auth by default. No scope granularity.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Airbyte OSS: free self-hosted; Cloud free tier: limited credits
pricing.paid_tiers: Airbyte Cloud: $1.50/credit; Team and Enterprise: custom
pricing.notes: Open source self-hosted is free. Cloud is credit-based per row synced. MCP server is open source.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits not prominently documented

known_agent_gotchas: Connection IDs (UUIDs) required for most operations — discover these first
Sync jobs are async — agents must poll for job completion
Cloud vs OSS have different API base URLs and authentication
Connector configuration schema varies by source — inspect before creating connections
API key lacks scope granularity — full workspace access
Large catalogs with many streams require careful stream selection
agent_readiness.security_notes: Cloud enforces HTTPS; self-hosted requires TLS configuration. API key lacks scopes. SOC 2, GDPR. Self-hosted is operator's security responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: Cloud enforces HTTPS. No scope granularity. SOC 2, GDPR for cloud. Self-hosted TLS is operator responsibility.
